description: Secret handling and credential protection rules. 20 rules governing secret storage, injection, rotation, and output scrubbing.

rules.description: NEVER write secrets, API keys, tokens, or credentials to any output file or tool result
rules.rationale: Secrets in output files are permanently exposed and often committed to version control

rules.description: NEVER expose secrets in environment variables accessible to sandbox or LLM context
rules.rationale: Environment variables are visible to all processes and frequently logged

rules.description: ALWAYS inject secrets via file descriptor or tmpfs mount, never via environment variable
rules.rationale: File descriptor injection is the only method that keeps secrets out of process environment

rules.description: NEVER send secrets to external models (Gemini, OpenAI, or any third-party API)
rules.rationale: Secrets sent to external services are permanently compromised and out of your control

rules.description: ALWAYS scrub tool output for secrets using regex + Shannon entropy before returning to LLM context
rules.rationale: LLM context can retain secrets across turns, creating persistent exposure

rules.description: NEVER store secrets in the Obsidian vault or memory-guard persistent storage
rules.rationale: Persistent storage retains secrets beyond the session lifetime

rules.description: ALWAYS rotate short-lived secrets automatically after each session or on compromise detection
rules.rationale: Short-lived secrets limit the blast radius of any single compromise

rules.description: NEVER allow the LLM to read the secret-manager database or master key
rules.rationale: Secret manager state is the crown jewel; LLM access = total secret compromise

rules.description: NEVER hardcode secrets in generated code, configuration files, or documentation
rules.rationale: Hardcoded secrets in code/docs are the most common source of credential leaks

rules.description: ALWAYS use HashiCorp Vault or Kubernetes Secrets for production secret storage
rules.rationale: Production requires enterprise-grade secret management with audit and rotation

rules.description: NEVER log secret values even at DEBUG level — log key names only
rules.rationale: Logs are frequently aggregated to central systems with different access controls

rules.description: ALWAYS apply minimum 128-bit encryption for secrets at rest
rules.rationale: Industry standard for secret encryption at rest

rules.description: NEVER allow secret values in error messages or stack traces
rules.rationale: Error messages are frequently displayed to users and logged without filtering

rules.description: ALWAYS validate secret references before injection (ensure secret exists and is current)
rules.requirement: secret_exists == true and not_expired == true and not_revoked == true
rules.rationale: Prevents injection of stale or revoked secrets that could cause auth failures

rules.description: ALWAYS include secret access in the attestation chain with key name (not value) and timestamp
rules.requirement: access_logged == true and key_name_recorded == true and value_never_recorded == true
rules.rationale: Secret access must be auditable without exposing the secret itself

rules.description: NEVER allow the LLM to request creation of new secrets or credentials
rules.rationale: LLM-generated credentials lack proper provisioning workflow and audit trail

rules.description: NEVER reuse the same secret across different skills or sessions without explicit approval
rules.rationale: Secret sharing across boundaries increases blast radius

rules.description: ALWAYS detect and block high-entropy strings that match secret patterns in all output
rules.rationale: Entropy-based detection catches secrets that evade regex patterns

rules.description: NEVER allow secrets to be included in memory-guard's federated memory sharing
rules.rationale: Cross-instance sharing of secrets violates the principle of least distribution

rules.description: ALWAYS quarantine and alert when secret leakage is detected in any output channel
rules.rationale: Secret leakage requires immediate containment and human review

rules.description: ALWAYS mount K8s secrets labeled kimi-sandbox-accessible as read-only files at /run/secrets/
rules.rationale: Resolves secret injection paradox: K8s RBAC grants secrets access only to labeled secrets, mounted read-only via file descriptor per SEC-003
